summary: Micro-CT lacks validated reliability in depicting true microstructures like trabecular architecture or cell-level details of maxillary alveolar bone; histology remains superior for observing actual microscopic anatomical features. The available evidence shows no confirmation of sufficient spatial resolution required for accurate representation of microscale bone characteristics.
